page: Amazon Web Services r6id.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top 25% results in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory bandwidth tests, including both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes, consistently rank in the top 25%. Cryptographic capabilities are notable, with the PassMark CPU encryption test reaching the top 10%, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES and VAES. Single-core performance, including Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core tests, remains in the average tier.

This hardware profile is configured for memory-intensive workloads that require high-capacity local storage, such as large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and data indexing. The inclusion of 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage paired with 768.0 GB of RAM offers high resource density, reducing the need for external network-attached storage for high-throughput database operations. This resource density provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads constrained by memory capacity and local I/O speed, though the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy parallel model training.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 25% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and PassMark benchmarks, with its cryptographic performance reaching the top 10%. While single-core metrics remain in the average tier, its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage capacity make it highly efficient for memory-intensive database operations, in-memory caching, and data processing workloads that require low-latency local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database benchmark performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform combining ninety-six virtual cores with high-capacity local NVMe storage for demanding database workloads.

768.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per physical core
5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance

Suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data indexing
